talk_metadata.talk_title: MedGamma fine‑tuning and federated QLoRA results across four cancer centers
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: we can see that even with the kind of smaller QLoRA weights, the model performance at extraction of these variables significantly improves.
Speaker said: federated runs are actually planned for this week.

key_data_gaps: No external validation on independent sites
No long‑term outcome or survival data presented

crispro_opportunity.description: The team acknowledges that ~20% of model behavior may not be captured by sparse autoencoders, indicating a need for deeper interpretability tools.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: we are aware that roughly 20% of model behavior may not be captured by these sparse autoencoders and embeddings carry some immersion risks.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can provide a transparent, causal attribution framework to map model decisions to specific clinical concepts, reducing the unexplained 20% gap.

crispro_opportunity.description: Current differential privacy implementation is being explored; stronger privacy guarantees could accelerate site onboarding.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: we are actively investigating and exploring other privacy‑preserving methods that's not going to sacrifice a lot of... trade‑offs from the model accuracy.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO’s privacy‑preserving federated analytics can quantify and mitigate leakage risk, providing measurable privacy budgets for each site.

unresolved_questions: How will the interpretability layer reliably identify which clinical concepts drive trial‑matching decisions?
What is the quantitative impact of the 20% unexplained model behavior on downstream clinical decisions?

talk_metadata.talk_title: Federated survival prediction for glioblastoma using MRIs and foundation models
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: these results are maybe not at the level that would support clinical decision‑making as is.
Speaker said: we are exploring additional modalities and longitudinal data to improve performance.

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Model relies only on a single pre‑operative MRI snapshot, missing longitudinal tumor evolution and treatment effects.

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Predict overall survival in GBM from pre‑operative MRI
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: No requirement for longitudinal imaging or molecular markers, limiting predictive power across heterogeneous patient cohorts.
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Molecular subtype (e.g., MGMT methylation, IDH status) not incorporated.
trial_dilution_risk.static_vs_dynamic_failure: No serial imaging or biomarker updates during training.
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Sample size limited to three public datasets plus 104 private patients; may be underpowered for robust survival modeling.

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Reliance on a single pre‑trained encoder (Brain SegFounder) without proprietary enhancements.
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker noted the encoder is publicly available; competitors could adopt the same model architecture.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Pursuing a broad, all‑comers GBM model rather than stratifying by molecular subtypes may dilute signal.

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that the model achieves consistent Kaplan‑Meier separation across leave‑one‑site‑out cohorts, then concluded that the results are not yet at a level to support clinical decision‑making.

key_data_gaps: No longitudinal imaging or treatment data
No external validation on independent cohorts

crispro_opportunity.description: Incorporate molecular biomarkers (e.g., MGMT, IDH) into the federated model to improve survival prediction.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: we are planning expansions for including radiology reports, other imaging types and additional clinical markers which have shown great synergy.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate multi‑omics and imaging features within a unified federated framework, enabling biomarker‑driven risk stratification.

crispro_opportunity.description: Add longitudinal MRI timepoints and treatment data to the federated training set to capture dynamic tumor evolution.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: the model is based on a single time point preoperative MRI that has obvious limitations when it comes to especially long‑term prediction power.
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO’s time‑aware federated learning pipelines can handle sequential imaging data while preserving privacy.

unresolved_questions: How will incorporation of additional modalities affect federated model convergence and privacy guarantees?
What is the optimal aggregation method beyond simple FedAvg for heterogeneous GBM data?
